prompt: Create an ultra-detailed, photorealistic yet hyperreal travel-poster image of a fantastical Japanese regional airport terminal that combines Toyama and Takayama tourism into one overwhelming landmark. The main building is a glass-and-steel airport façade labeled in large Japanese and English text “富山高山すし空港” and “TOYAMA TAKAYAMA SUSHI AIRPORT,” with two dark entrance boards below reading “出発 DEPARTURES” and “到着 ARRIVALS.” Above the terminal, build a giant rooftop sushi display with exactly 5 oversized nigiri pieces: tuna, salmon, shrimp, fatty tuna, and another red fish, with a large gold “すし” sign in front and stylized blue ocean waves around it. Behind it, add a sign reading “立山連峰 / TATEYAMA MOUNTAIN RANGE,” and fill the entire background with dramatic snow-covered Japanese Alps under a clear blue sky. Show exactly 2 airplanes: one large plane nose emerging behind the sushi sign near the center-left, and one passenger jet flying at the upper right with Japanese lettering on the fuselage. Include exactly 1 airport control tower on the right side. Surround the airport with dense, colorful regional festival and food imagery: a gassho-zukuri thatched-roof house at upper left, a red-and-black cable car at far left, ornate Takayama festival floats with lanterns and dolls, Daruma dolls, vertical banners, seafood signs, and souvenir-shop-like storefronts. Add exactly 6 prominent regional attraction or food signboards around the façade: “白えび” with a shrimp illustration, “ますのすし” with a round salmon sushi cross-section, “富山湾 TOYAMA BAY,” “氷見ブリ HIMI BURI,” “黒部ダム KUROBE DAM,” and “飛騨牛 HIDA BEEF” with a black cow head and a slab of marbled beef. Put many travelers with suitcases and crowds in front of the entrance for scale. Use saturated colors, crisp daylight, extreme detail, commercial tourism-advertising composition, wide 16:9 aspect ratio, no blank areas, no watermark.
